statement: The grammar pattern 前に means 'before [action]'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '前に' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+まえに, Noun+の+まえに. before going to the UK
exampleSentence: あなたが去る前には火を確実に消しなさい。 (Be sure to put out the fire before you leave.)

statement: The grammar pattern くらい means 'about, as'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'くらい' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: Pattern: Noun+くらい/ぐらい. (about) as normal as any other guy on the street.
exampleSentence: 人に会ったら、あいさつぐらいして欲しい (When I meet people I'd like at least a hello.)

statement: The grammar pattern 下さい means 'please'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '下さい' mean?
explanation: Pattern: て-form+ください, を+ください. Please give me that
exampleSentence: それをください。 (That, please .)

statement: The grammar pattern ばかり means 'just (just recently) / nothing but'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'ばかり' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: Pattern: Verb casual past た-form+ばかり, Verb て-form+ばかり, Verb dictionary form+ばかり, Noun+ばかり. I just bought it / you do nothing but play around
exampleSentence: その本を買ったばかりだ。 (I just bought that book)

statement: The grammar pattern たら means 'if, when (verb form)'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'たら' mean?
distractors: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
explanation: Pattern: Verb past た-form+ら. When I wake up
exampleSentence: その夜半に目覚めたら呼吸が酷く不規則で苦しくなっていました。 (When (I) woke up late that night (my) breathing had become horribly irregular and difficult.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜ね means 'isn't it?'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜ね' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Sentence+ね. Isn't this nice!
exampleSentence: これはいいですね。 (Isn't this nice !)

statement: The grammar pattern ないように means 'to avoid'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'ないように' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: to avoid; to prevent
exampleSentence: 指を切らないようにして下さい。 (Please make sure you do not cut your finger.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜だ means 'The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜だ' mean?
correctAnswer: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
distractors: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
explanation: Pattern: Sentence+だ. I'm English.
exampleSentence: 犬だ！ (It's a dog!)

statement: The grammar pattern 少し means 'a bit...'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '少し' mean?
explanation: A bit cold
exampleSentence: かれは少しおそいですね。 (He's a bit late isn't he?)

statement: The grammar pattern すぎる means 'too; too much; excessively'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'すぎる' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: Pattern: Verb stem+すぎる, な-adjective (without な)+すぎる, い-adjective (without い)+すぎる. This problem is too difficult for me
exampleSentence: その人はちょっと食べすぎましたね。 (That person ate a little too much .)

statement: The grammar pattern たり する　form means 'doing things like V+ing'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'たり する　form' mean?
explanation: Yesterday I did things like watching tv and playing video games.
exampleSentence: 飲んだり、食べたり、歌ったり、ダンスをしたり、大いに楽しみました。 (We drank, ate, sung, danced - we generally had one big fun time!)

statement: The grammar pattern そう means 'Looks like'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'そう' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Verb ます-stem+そう+(だ), い-adjective without い+そう+(だ), な-adjective (without な)+そう+(だ), Negative form ~ない without い+さ+そう+(だ). Looks like snow will fall
exampleSentence: これは、食べられそうではありません。 (This doesn't look like it's edible.)

statement: The grammar pattern がる means 'to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'がる' mean?
correctAnswer: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: Pattern: い adjective (without い)+がる, な adjective+がる. He (looks like) he wants a new car.
exampleSentence: 小川さんはどうも新しい車を欲しがるんですね。 (Mr. Ogawa really looks like he wants a new car, doesn't he?)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜始める means 'to begin/start VERB-ing'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜始める' mean?
distractors: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
explanation: Pattern: Verb stem+始める. I started reading yesterday.
exampleSentence: ６時を過ぎると従業員は帰り始めた。 (After 6 p.m. the employees began to  return home.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜は means ''topic marker' (also shown as 'wa')'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜は' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: X wa Y desu : X is Y
exampleSentence: 田中さんは大学生です。 (Mr. Tanaka is a university student.)

statement: The grammar pattern なので means 'since'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'なので' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: since this is secret
exampleSentence: この情報は内々に知らされたものなので、あなたに何も教えることはできない (This information was given to me in confidence, so I can't tell you anything.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜など means 'and etc.'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜など' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
explanation: and so on
exampleSentence: 彼は失敗などしないと言う。 (He says he is above failure.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜も means '(with -ve) neither ... nor , also not, either'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜も' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: That isn't mine either.
exampleSentence: コーヒーも紅茶も飲みません。 (I drink neither coffee nor tea.)

statement: The grammar pattern どうして means 'why?, what for?'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'どうして' mean?
explanation: Why didn't you come?
exampleSentence: どうしてそんなことが起こったのか。 (How did such a thing come about?)

statement: The grammar pattern けれども means 'but, however'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'けれども' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: It rained, but everybody enjoyed themselves.
exampleSentence: 雨が降っていたけれども、みんなは楽しい時を過ごした。 (Although it rained, everyone had a good time.)

statement: The grammar pattern でも means 'but'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'でも' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: I'd like to make it easier to use but still not use frames.
exampleSentence: 品質はいい。でも、値段は高いです。 (The quality is good but the price is high.)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜か means '? (basic question particle)'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜か' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Sentence+か. Is this your book?
exampleSentence: これはあなたの本ですか。 (Is this your book ?)

statement: The grammar pattern 〜へ means 'indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e''
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '〜へ' mean?
correctAnswer: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: I will go abroad next year.
exampleSentence: 私は来年外国へ行きます (I will go abroad next year.)

statement: The grammar pattern ので means 'because, and'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'ので' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: Pattern: Verb (casual)+ので, い-adjective+ので, な-adjective+な+ので, Noun+な+ので.
exampleSentence: 彼の話はあまりにも馬鹿げていたので誰も信じなかった。 (His story was too ridiculous for anyone to believe.)

statement: The grammar pattern ないで means 'without'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'ないで' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: Pattern: Verb nai form+で. "I went to school without eating breakfast."
exampleSentence: 朝ご飯を食べないで学校へ行った。 (I went to school without eating breakfast.)

statement: The grammar pattern より means 'more ____'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'より' mean?
explanation: Pattern: より+Something. Don't you know a better method?
exampleSentence: よりいい方法知りませんか。 (Don't you know a better method?)

statement: The grammar pattern つもり (積もり・積り) means 'One's intention; conviction; belief'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'つもり (積もり・積り)' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
explanation: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+つもり+(だ), Verb nai form+つもり+(だ), Verb dictionary form+つもり+だった, Verb nai form+つもり+だった. I intend to do that; I am convinced that ...
exampleSentence: 私は医者になるつもりです。 (I intend to become  a doctor.)

statement: The grammar pattern 欲しい means 'to want something (desirative)'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '欲しい' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Verb て-form+ほしい, Noun+が+ほしい. I want a car.
exampleSentence: 靴がほしいのです。 (I'd like some shoes.)

statement: The grammar pattern 頂きます means 'to receive'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '頂きます' mean?
distractors: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
exampleSentence: 頂きます。 (Thanks for the food (Before eating))

statement: The grammar pattern から〜まで means 'from ... until'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'から〜まで' mean?
explanation: from spring until autumn
exampleSentence: 今年の秋から来年まで3Gが主戦場になる (From this Autumn to next year 3G will be the central battlefield)

statement: The grammar pattern しかし (然し・併し) means 'However, but'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern 'しかし (然し・併し)' mean?
distractors: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
explanation: But now things have changed a lot.
exampleSentence: しかし、私はあなたが大好きですよ。 (However , I really like you.)

statement: The grammar pattern 以上 means 'exceeding, above'
quizQuestion: What does the grammar pattern '以上' mean?
explanation: Pattern: Noun (quantity)+以上. More than two hours
exampleSentence: あなたは必要以上のカロリーを摂っている。 (You take more calories than necessary.)
